div.h2: conn_t Struct Memory Layout — Cache Line Boundaries

div.p: sizeof(conn_t) = 4112 bytes Cache lines (64 B each): 64.25 — spans 65 cache lines total. Cold first access: ~64 cache-miss loads from DRAM (≈200 cycles each). Hot access: state+fd in L1; read_buf in L2/L3. Growth direction: low → high address (top to bottom as shown).
⚑ Hot fields: state + fd land here First access = 1 cache miss → loads state, fd, and first 56B of read_buf
62 full cache lines + partial tails Cold access pattern: reading large messages sequentially prefetched by hardware prefetcher. Random access (mid-buffer parse) = L3 miss.
Shares cache line 64 with end of struct. Accessing read_len after read_buf tail = separate cache miss if buffer is cold. 8-byte aligned — no struct padding needed.
